name: Central Bank of Nigeria
statute: Risk-Based Cybersecurity Framework & Guidelines for OFIs / PSPs (2022)

obligations.title: Board-approved cybersecurity policy
obligations.description: Maintain a board-approved, annually reviewed cybersecurity policy aligned with the CBN Risk-Based Cybersecurity Framework for Other Financial Institutions.

obligations.title: Appoint a Chief Information Security Officer
obligations.description: Designate a qualified CISO reporting to executive management, with defined authority over cybersecurity controls and incident response.

obligations.title: Annual cyber risk assessment
obligations.description: Conduct and document a cybersecurity risk assessment annually; feed findings into the board's risk appetite statement.

obligations.title: Report cyber incidents to CBN within 24 hours
obligations.description: Report material cyber incidents (service disruption, data compromise, fraud loss thresholds) to the CBN Incident Response Team within 24 hours of detection.

obligations.title: Independent penetration testing
obligations.description: Commission independent penetration testing of critical systems at least annually and remediate Critical/High findings within the mandated windows.

obligations.title: Tested business continuity & disaster recovery plan
obligations.description: Maintain and test BCP/DR plans at least annually, including failover to the DR site for critical services.

obligations.title: Risk-based KYC / customer due diligence
obligations.description: Operate risk-based customer identification and due diligence consistent with the CBN AML/CFT/CPF Regulations and BVN/NIN verification rules.

obligations.title: File Suspicious Transaction Reports with NFIU
obligations.description: Detect, escalate, and file STRs with the Nigerian Financial Intelligence Unit within 24 hours of forming a suspicion.
